title: Great villas, exceptional food
review_text: We were anxious to see the Bulgari hotel, having stayed at two other resorts in Bali and everyone talking about it. There was a slight concern about the service level since the hotel just opened a few weeks ago. The facilities of the villas are outstanding. Plunge pool, outside terraces and space, GREAT food and restaurants. It has a different feel than the Amanresorts. If you are looking for true quietness, you might be better off at one of the Amans'. However, the Bulgari manages to bring in a stylish feeling to Bali which one probably cannot find anywhere else, without destroying the Bali style and spirit. To me, a perfect mix! The service was good but not perfect as is to be expected at such a new resort. I m not someone who stops the watch to see how quick my breakfast is here...so I had no issues at all. My wife and I spent 2 full days just at the villa it was so nice without leaving it. It is not cheap but if one looks for a stylish alternative to the Aman's go for it !

title: Stylish, slick but not quite what you expect
review_text: My husband and I stayed here for 4 nights in June and had a beautiful stay. The hotel grounds are amazing and the suites are incredibly stylish and well appointed. We found the GM and the other senior managers most welcoming and ready to give a superior service but this didn't quite trickle down to the staff. The Balinese staff are wonderful but their training needs to be more polished. Breakfast we waited 10 minutes to get served, with three other couples in the restaurant, and the inability to get a cup of tea which had left to brew astounded me with the price that the cup of tea costs. Slightly frustrating. For the first two days it was buffet then they decided to change it to a-la carte??? The poolside menu is pretentious, who wants a Wagyu beef burger or a pizza with foie grass on it? Simplicity is so much more stylish. The poolside menu is so expensive it is a bit off putting. Sunset cocktails are a must, the strawberry caprioska's are divine, and with this they serve 7 mini dishes from the Italian restaurant. This is worth it. The restaurants are really nice but again who wants lobster in their nasi goreng, not me. I see their luxury angle but it is a bit lost on me when there are so many good restaurants in Bali which are into simple, organic healthy food. There were a number of cashed of people who were drinking red wine in 90% humidity who obviously thought that this is what the rich do???? I understand champagne,chilled white wine or cocktails but a bottle of Grange in 36 degree heat, yuck. The spa is divine. I was in an ocean view suite and the scrub and massge was really good worth the money. We will go back there but we do get better service at a hotel whihc is not nearly as expensive as has so many restaurants shopping etc around it. It really is a couples only hotel. I would not go there with a group of friends. I hope that the staff have been better inducted and the menu has been simplified when we go back there.

title: Stunning....with a bit of attitude.
review_text: I have to caveat my review by saying that we did not spend the night here. We spent the day here at the spa and then had lunch. We were going to spend a night but were turned off by some of the staff and 'rules' so decided not to. More on that.... This property is stunning. Absolutely beautiful. Probably the most beautiful that I've ever seen actually. The spa is gorgeous and the 'relaxing' area is magnificent. We had the full package spa thing with a private villa. Do NOT pay for the private villa - it is not worth it. The spa services were ok...but the setting is really what you are going for...again, so stunning!!! We looked at a room and they were fabulous - no question. We went to the restaurant and it was fabulous with delicious food, drinks, service - just watch the beautiful sea go by. The pool was magnificent and this is really where the problem came in. We had just spent just as much as a room in their spa, there was 3 people by the pool (the hotel is empty) and when we went to go for a dip after having lunch and drinks (just 2 of us), we were told that the pool was off limits to us. Mind you, we were sitting about 10 yards from the pool. The whole thing just kind of turned us off...I completely understand that there must be policies and if the pool was crowded that this might be an issue...or if we had just showed up for lunch or a drink and then proceeded to camp out by the pool for the day...but, we had just spent almost $1K there and it was 3 or 4 in the afternoon. Anyway, this is a beautiful property and i would recommend one, maybe two nights here if you can afford it (not cheap). More than that would be too much I think.

title: Luxury at the end of the World
review_text: The Bvlgari Bali has a spectacular location on the cliff of the southern end of Uluwatu. You are greeted at the airy "lobby" and brought down to the actual resort by small golf cars. The grounds of the hotels are perfectly designed. Everything thaughtfully planned, without looking to perfect. The common pool is so beautiful, with several daybeds and sunloungers spread around. To get to the beach you need to take a small cable car, quite steep, but when you get down to the beach, you are standing with on one of the most picture perfect beaches in the world!!! The villas are beautiful, with a good layout and beautiful decorated with furniture by B&B Italia. Each villa has a small plunge pool, all with the same spectacular view. Only thing is that the villas are a little dark inside... I was a little disappointed of the villas, you can experience the same at several other resorts around the island, to a much lower rate. The three restaurants served gourmet cuisine, and it was memorable to dine on the edge of the cliff with thousands of stars above you and the waves rolling on the beach 100 meters below. I missed my special one sitting there alone..... The spa treatment was one of the best ever! Among other treatments, I had a 4 hour session in a private open air pavillion, truly perfect! The spa lady had the special touch that separate mediate massage from the top notch! The treatments are pricey, but worth every rupiah! You go there to experience one of the finest resorts ever build, to worship the sun and get the perfect tan, to have fantastic spa treatments, to eat great food and to pamper yourself. The rate for my villa vwas around USD 900,-. There are no complimentary pick up at the airport, they charge you USD 115,- each way. The Bvlgari is also quite isolated, and you need to hire a driver to get around. Then you can start the discussion, is it worth it..... For a stay of 3/4 nights just to have the experience of the place, absolutely! For a 2 weeks holiday, no.....

title: Fine hotel but not worth the hype
review_text: I have been reading the glowing reviews of Bulgari and suspect it is from people who do not frequent Bali a lot. In short, it is a fine hotel but nothing particularly spectacular in virtually any respect (rooms, service, location, etc.). We had a two bedroom villa with a central livingroom. It was fine. The view was spectacular. The restaurant was typical for Bali with a mix of local and international cuisine - all fine. The service in the restaurants was quite attentive to the needs of our children. The beach (although really not swimmable) is something to behold - we were there with the family for an hour without seeing another soul! In short, everything was fine but it is not so different from the Four Seasons Jimbaran but about 2X the price. As a family that returns to Bali every 3 months, we would not return to this hotel. Check out The Samaya in Seminyak (cool and trendy), the Four Seasons in Jimbaran (great for families) or any of the Amans.

title: Probably one of the top 5 in Bali, but definitely not the best
review_text: Before starting this review, I'd like to say that I have been to Bali in July/August 8 times already, in good conditions, and that I have been to many high end hotels and resorts on the island. I had heard many positive comments/reviews concerning the Bulgari and I was rather excited to stay in this hotel for 4 nights. However I left with a contrasted impression due to several points I will now develop. Pros: -The situation is pretty decent, as the view is definitely breathtaking (one of the nicest in Bali), and the hotel is located less than half an hour away from the International Airport. -The hotel itself is design and, as it is only 34 months old, it is still in good condition. The decoration is modern and more tasteful than at the Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay. -There are two very nice boutiques located within the hotel (they are pretty expensive though). -The breakfast, which is a hybrid between "all you can eat" buffet and a la carte, is delicious and offers a sumptuous view. -The bathroom is beautiful, spacious and clean. It contains 2 showers (outdoor and indoor) and a large bathtub, plus two sinks. Cons: -The service is OK, but definitely not worth the price. Service at Four Seasons Sayan/JImbaran Bay, Alila, any of the Aman hotels is clearly better, personnel is more attentive. A few examples: the buggy (which is indispensable to any movement in the hotel) once took 20 minutes to arrive at my room while "5 to 7 minutes" is announced; the staff at the restaurants is not as professional as in Mozaic, Cire or the restaurant of the Amandari for instance; we had booking issues at the restaurant because our butler forgot to make the reservation for us... -The food at the Italian restaurant, which is supposed to be the best of its kind on the island, is overpriced and not that great. -The beach is nice but it is almost impossible to go swim: the ground is full of rocks and the waves are more of the "surfing caliber" - high and dangerous. -The 2 rooms I've been into were infected with armies of tiny ants. I found some in my bag, inside my laptop... Some came under my keyboard (annoying), and BEHIND my screen (very annoying). In total, I probably killed around 40 before giving up and asking for some bug spray. -The cliffside villas have a great view but the ones that are located higher offer a pretty mediocre view. -The rooms are poorly lit. Huge windows compensate during the day, but the room is somber after sunset. To conclude, overall Bulgari is a classy and sumptuous 5 star hotel. However I would not recommend it to a friend as some other comparable hotels that offer relatively similar prestations are slightly better... and cheaper. I am thinking about the brand new Alila which is extremely design (almost making Bulgari look traditional), offers a better service and serves tastier food for a couple hundred dollars less per night. I would also recommend the Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay, which is very similar to Bulgari (although older) but offers a truly professional service at a lower price.

title: Very luxurious Villa, average food
review_text: I agree with the 2 previous posts, and just want to add that the Italian Restaurant was uninspired and pretentious, while the asian one was serving great food. We had a villa on the second row, but still with a nice sea view, the transfer to the beach is spectacular, and we had a mile long walk without encountering anyone. (but it is not possible to swim in the ocean) The decoration and furniture of the villa and the bathroom was high-end ,but a little bit too dark,and the bed was too soft. (with spring noises) On one day we were served a mediocre breakfast with canned juice, they apologised and took it off the bill, on the second day breakfast was perfect. Great service from Agung our personal butler. They charge 60 US $ + tax for the airport drop off in their cheapest car, taxi was 8,50 US$

title: Posh resort
review_text: Security at the resort is very high, as soon as we got to the gates, our car was checked by 2 guards and our luggage by watch dogs! We thought this was too much. Other than that, we thought the resort was incredibly beautiful, luxurious and extravagant in every way. Ocean Villa Our villa faced the sea, however the Spa opposite to our villa blocked a portion of the view (make sure your villa is unobstructed). The villa is furnished extravagantly with white and black being the dominant colors. The bed is the most comfortable bed you'll ever sleep on; the sheets and pillows were of the highest quality too. The bathroom is very modern and there is an oversized bath-tub in the centre. There are indoor and outdoor showers with Bvlgari toiletries. There is a complimentary beach bag for you and your spouse including a straw hat and a kite. The garden in the villa is well landscaped and contains a lovely seating and dining area. The private pool is nice but small and the water was a little too cold to swim in. We didnt feel that our villa was private as people passing the hill behind the spa can look into our pool and tanning beds. However, it was still amazing to sit and tan all day long with an incredible view of the sea a few meters ahead. And the great thing was that we didnt see a single bug, which was amazing as Bali is a tropical island. Service The service was good and the staff were friendly, knowing that we were from the middle east, they provided two middle eastern music CD's in the room specially for us. Although it is mentioned that there is a host to every villa, we didnt hear or see much of our host. We had to make our own restaurant reservations, arrange the buggy and call front desk to inquire about activities and places to visit. Thus we didnt feel the service was personal. Food The food was amazing, really tasty! The breakfast was great and we also had the in-villa dining a couple of times which was fresh, and really tasty. I tried the Balinese food so cant comment on the Italian or others. Spa The Bvlgari spa is the most beautiful spa I've ever seen from an architectural point of view. I just had a hair cut there, which turned out well but took too long. All in all, we had a wonderful stay but thought that the rooms were grossly overpriced, USD1000 for the lowest category room was definitely steep. Also, the overly posh atmosphere had us on edge sometimes and so we werent completely relaxed. I would only go again of they reduced their rates by half or had really good offers although I still do recommend this hotel for a couple of days but not more.

title: Jaw-dropping in every aspect
review_text: Went to the Bulgari for our friends wedding reception. Beautiful setting (Apertivo at the Bar, followed by seven course feast at Sangkar, then drinks), spotless service. Villas (we took a peak at our friend's villa) were sophisticated and elegant. The property exceeded itself in ensuring that everyone had a great time when our friends waved off to their happily ever after. Everything in the property was jaw-dropping, from the views, cliff, service and food, along with the most expensive stuff you can find in a hotel gift shop (a 182,000 USD Watch perhaps? hehe)

title: Visited for dinner, stunning resort!
review_text: In my opinion the resorts staff and public areas are the best! The views are amazing, the bar is fantastic and every staff member is impressive. It is a shame that they have a reputation for small villas & small plunge pool. If you can get a good rate, I'd definately stay here to experience the restaurants, views etc.

title: Nice, but not exceptionally nice
review_text: We stayed there for a 4-day trip. Everything was amazing -- from the moment you stepped out from the plane to the moment they sent you back to the airport. Once we stepped out from the plane, we were picked up by 2 random men who escorted us to the immigration and did the visa for us. I have no idea where these people from since our holiday package includes 'pick-up from airport' but we didn't expect they would literally pick us up from the plane and did all the visa/luggage for us. Soon after, we were greeted by another woman outside the airport and a luxurious 7-seater car was waiting for us. There're two bottles of chilled acqua panna plus cold towels -- I felt like the Queen of England. We arrived the resort after a short ride. We waited a while in the reception area -- although with the breathtaking view, I guess the check-in process could go quicker. We checked in villa no.59 and were assigned with a butler. The villa itself -- nice but smaller than I expected. For a start, there's no living room so we spent most of our evenings lying in the bed because there's no sofa indoor. There's a pavilion with a dining table and sofa but our food got eaten by monkeys (they did warn us, but I underestimated the monkeys' intelligence, they actually ripped the cling film and ate the fruits!) and there're too many bugs around so not possible to chill out in the evening. The pool -- nice but again small. I ended up going to the main pool for a proper swim -- but if you are not a keen swimmer and simply looking for a pool to stretch your limbs, I guess it's alright. The bathroom -- something I can't complain. It's HUGE and so lavish. The bath tub was placed in the middle of the bathroom and I felt true luxury -- no better place for a self-indulgence retreat. There's a B&O TV in the room and we went down to their DVD library to pick few movies. Sad thing is that their DVD library stocks what I suspect pirate copies of DVD and the machine could not play 90% of the DVDs. As such I guess I can't blame the resort's management because we went down to the mall in town and the DVD shop sold something like pirate copies there as well. So perhaps it's difficult to get genuine copies in Bali. FOOD -- I still miss every dish they serve and would definitely recommend everyone in Bali to go there and try. We had almost every meal with the Sangkar restaurant and a splurge meal with their Italian restaurant. So nice that you have nothing to complain about. Words can't describe how nice the foods were -- you just have to try out by yourself. We didn't try out the spa because it's pricey. We didn't go down to the beach either because we saw the beach being quite rocky so preferred staying next to the pool. I would recommend the resort to my friends but have already planned to stay with the new banyan tree (the ungasan resort) and the Alila Uluwatu for my next visit to Bali.

title: We still can't stop talking about that view!
review_text: The Bulgari was our last hotel stay of 3 on our 2 week honeymoon to Bali. It easily had the best view, dare i say best view of any hotel we had ever been (if you like staring at clear blue ocean from atop a very tall sheer cliff). The villa was very nice, very big bedroom with a super comfortable bed and views out to the Indian Ocean. How amazing it was to wake up every morning to that. Huge bathroom with lots of nice ammenities and very soothing rain shower. Excellent service from the hotel staff as well. All that said the cost of the food almost ruins it...it's prohibitively expensive! Even for a high end resort and for someone who has eaten in some of the finest restaurants. I agree with the reviewer who said $40 for nasi goreng was ridiculous (basically fried rice). Yes it has lobster but it's just as good with chicken and you can probably slash the price in half and at least give your guests another option. And yes, we realize wine is expensive in Bali but we saw no bottles under $300 USD on the menu and that was the cheap bad stuff. On our last day in Bali we ventured over to the Alila for lunch and it blew away the food at the Bulgari...and was MUCH cheaper too. Beautiful hotel, would stay again but try to eat somewhere else unless you have very very deep pockets.

title: Picturesque view for chillaxing holiday
review_text: I stayed at the hotel with my family, we are lucky as we made the booking early before the trip and got one of the few family villas there. The villa has 2 spacious separate bedrooms with both indoor and outdoor bathrooms at each room (master bedroom has a decent size study area), 1 spacious living room, and a small edgeless pool. The room has a very nice scent and toiletries in the bathroom are all Bvlgari products. Customer and butler services are excellent. However as with many Asian countries, due to the humid weather you are likely to find insects/bugs in the villa then again they do provide guests with insects repellent. Have a walk around and explore the villa, it has a picturesque and breathtaking view. Go to the restaurants to have a peek at the views the villa has to offer. Take the inclined escalator (it's at about 45 angle and few hundred meters down the cliff - fun fun fun!!) to enjoy view of the Indian ocean and sea breeze. Restaurants and bars at the villa are indeed expensive but which other luxurious hotels/resorts are not. If you are after a luxurious holiday, it's worth a try staying in Bulgari Bali for 1-2 nights and just spend your time at the villa. If you'd like to stay longer in Bali, then you may want to stay at a different place for the rest of your stay. Overall, we had a great stay there and definitely love our chillaxing time there! Piece of advice: The villas are at the cliffs and there are wild monkeys from time to time. Usually the monkeys don't interrupt the guests, but to be safe close your bedroom and living room doors at all times especially when there are food in the room!

title: Stylish resort in a beautiful setting
review_text: We recently spent 4 nights at the Bulgari resort and didn't leave the resort once. We stayed in a cliff top villa which had beautiful views over the ocean. The room was very well appointed and we had our own plunge pool which was very private. It is quite a long way from the busy shopping and restaurant areas of Bali which can be good or bad depending on the type of holiday you are looking for. The beach restaurant had lovely fresh seafood in an idyllic location. The Italian restaurant in the resort also served beautiful food. There were some very nice touches in the service department. If you sat by the main pool, you were regularly served water and light snacks without feeling any pressure to buy food or drinks. The only slightly irritating aspect to the service was that when walking around the resort, every staff member you came across asked you what room you were staying in which gave you a feeling of surveillance. I'm not sure if this was done for security reasons or just to keep a track on when the guests were.

title: Honeymoon Happiness
review_text: My husband (newleyweds) and I travel a great deal and we were floored by this hotel. I rarely take the time to write a review on a hotel unless it's a poor one. The grounds and setting of the Bulgari are so spectacular. Upon entering the property, you will be instantly wowed. Our favorite part about this hotel is that every room is exceptional. Each room is it's own villa (with a pool), with plenty of room and a great view. Our least favorite part of the hotel are the high prices ($30 USD for sunscreen and $250-500 meals!). Once you are in the doors, it seems as though the hotel feels they can rip you off from everything like food to incidentals. Needless to say, it was an amazing place to experience our honeymoon. Don't expect to be immersed into Bali culture. If you're looking for culture, spend a few days in the jungle areas of Bali near Ubud then come to Bulgari. This is definitely a lay around and do nothing kind of resort. P.S. Disregard any reviews you read about bugs and lizards. . . there are bugs and lizards EVERYWHERE in Bali. I hate lizards and it's just something you have to deal with - ha! But we didn't have any in our room, thank god!

title: Stunning location & villas but dubious food...
review_text: I have read reports in this website complaining about the villas and the food.. hence, I was hesitant about making a reservation especially during the wet monsoon period. Yet, I did so as Bulgari is flexible about the minimum number of nights stay (some top resorts in Thailand, like Six Senses & Trisara insist on a 10 day minimum in peak periods giving you a USD 50k bill!).. and I love Bali anyway... I felt that the criticisms in Tripadvisor about the villas was totally unjustified. Some complain about the design of the villa (one review below talks about the lack of a covered walkway to the villa door entrance, but that is absurd as there is such a device called an "umbrella"..) and others complain about the wear & tear (another reviewer talks about the scratches on the bedroom floor but guess what? Wood scratches!). In reality, the villas are intelligently designed and well built. We stayed at a premier ocean view villa (number 45) and the sea-view was magnificent. Having stayed at all the Amans in Bali as well as the 4 Seasons Jimbaran, I would say that Bulgari's are the best beach villas. The layout maximizes the use of space both for the interior as well as the exterior, and the plunge pool is usable and attractive. All in all, the villas are superb (so please readers, don't whine about minor shortcomings!). Having said how much I loved the villas, I must also admit that the food was pretty disappointing. The breakfast buffet is rather pathetic with limited options. And why have 2 tiered pricing for those who want it with eggs (American) and those who don't (Continental)? I also ordered the breakfast nasi goreng and they used brown rice which is very bizarre (having lived in Asia, I know my fried rice and rice HAS to be fluffy and white). We also stayed for New Year and food there was similarly uninspiring, especially for the hefty price of the New Year's eve event! Thus, overall, I agree with the criticisms on this website about the quality of the food and the hotel really needs to look at a new F&B team - as the hotel is a long drive from anywhere else (apart from the Alila, which has better food) you don't have many other dining options. Also, the wine list is TOTALLY horrific in prices.. it is totally unjustified to charge guests hundreds of bucks (minimum) for a bottle of wine, and even more for champagne!! In spite of the criticisms of food (and cost of wine), this place is a "must see/stay/do" in Bali. The resort is well laid out, amenities are great (even the non-swimmable beach is pretty), service is excellent and the management team are very nice! Uluwatu is an authentic part of Bali that has not been destroyed by mass market tourists, and the Bulgari is in a stunning location. Combine it with a stay at the superb 4 Seasons in Ubud/Sayan and you have the perfect trip to the land of Eat, Pray, Love!

title: Perfect Romantic hideaway
review_text: If cost not a matter and you looking for a perfect hideaway, this is highly recommended place to stay no longer than 3 nights. The location is at the cliff of Uluwatu, south of Bali Island facing Indian Ocean. I and my wife stayed end of January 2012 when the weather was not very good. Windy, rain every day and very strong current. Beside the weather, this resorts was excellent. Services is at the top, food was OK, Villa quite well maintaned. Beware of monkeys, you should bever leave anything outside your room.

title: resort
review_text: excellent!! I've stayed here for 2 days. the food is okay, but too expensive than another luxury restaurants or resort. they should consider the price is

title: Breathtaking Views, Intimate Remote Resort
review_text: This resort is luxury at its finest. Carved into a former rainforest, the resort is nestled atop a mountain with sweeping and dramatic vistas overlooking the beach. One must take a vernacular down to the private beach and bar area to access the serene and private each and Indian Ocean. Very remote and secluded, this resort is designed for those who desire to get away from it all, relax and stay within the confines of the resort. A butler escorts you around the property via golf cart and they discourage walking about as it is very steep. The grounds are pristine which is the opposite of most of the rest of the island. Staff was continuously sweeping and cleaning falling flower blooms and keeping common areas tidy. It wa sso clean and immaculate it almost didnt feel real. The bar/ restaurant was exquisite and the staff, food, and beverage offerings were of the highest quality. We lingered for 6 hours one day. The native monkeys remain on the property and become quite aggressive around food. The staff was not completely successful in shoooing them away. One even quickly snatched a breadstick off my plate. I was surprised to see this but one must remember it was built on a former rainforest and monkey sanctuary. It is a gorgous place and very romantic.

title: Luxury cant get better
review_text: Bulgari is a destination which is a perfect combination of luxury, functionality and comfort. I was of the opinion that Maldives is where the best resorts and views are but pleasantly surprised with Bulgari Bali. The rooms and food are excellent. The beach is not as beautiful as what you would see in maldives nevertheless the resort made up for it. Would love to be back soon.

title: Amazing!
review_text: We stayed at the Bulgari for our first 2 nights in Bali before moving to a more central hotel in Seminyak to be closer to shopping/ restaurants and to be next to a beach where we can swim as opposed to the beach by the Bulgari which is beautiful but you cannot swim in it. We now regret it because the beach was horrible, the hotel terrible and the town of Seminyak really not great, very dirty and in need of sidewalk and street repairs ASAP. We should have stayed secluded at the Bulgari. Our welcoming at the airport was smooth, we were assisted and quickly went through to obtain visas. We arrived at the resort late at night, we were greeted with a really good mojito to freshen up. We were then driven to our villa and wow! It was gorgeous! Great outdoor area with sitting and dining area as well as private infinity pool and lounge chairs overlooking the ocean with shades should you want to close off the area more although very private. The bedroom was very confortable, the bed linens amazing. We were welcomed with a private message from gm, fruits and dessert. The closet space was very spacious, his and hers separate closets. We were offered a beach bag and beach hats. The washroom was divine! Amazing bathtub and shower and an amazing outdoor shower. You wanted to shower more than twice a day! The amenities offered were so many from insect repellent, nail filer, brushes and much more. The Bulgari products are very good quality and the blow dryer was elchim. The only other hotel I know that elchim is the Petit Hotel in Montreal, an elchim blow dryer is a great touch for women. The breakfast was excellent, food quality, choice and service. The pool was very nice with amazing view and lovely flowers which were also all over the resort. You felt like you were in Italy! The service at the pool was excellent - amazing large towels, great drink service, got free bouchees every hour, sun products were also offered. The washrooms by the pool were also gorgeous. The beach is located low and you can get there by a great lift. The lounge area is very nice with sand. You can go on the beach, however it is not advised to swim. We took a long walk in the morning but at a point lage waves came and we had a hard time escaping them. The only thing this resort is missing is a great beach to lounge by all day. It is a gorgeous place to be, it would be only better if Bulgari built this hotel on an island with an amazing beach to complement this amazing resort. Bali was the wrong location for such a beautiful resort. We did not enjoy Bali and found it very overrated. The monkeys all over the resort were cute until they robbed our villa. We made sure to lock the windows and doors at all times, yet during our absence, a monkey made its way in through the roof which was not properly closed. Engineering and housekeeping immediately came to repair and clean up, however the incident took about an hour to be resolved so we did it get a chance to enjoy our pool as planned before dinner. Also, I did not find that the hotel did anything to compensate for the unpleasant experience given that the monkey got in due to an opening on the roof. On a negative note, we did not enjoy our massage at the spa. The welcoming was nice, however the bathrobes offered did not seem clean, they seemed to have been reused and the bed linens were not white, they had a pattern and did not look clean nor did they smell fresh. The treatment was not great - we were very surprised because they had received awards. We booked a 90 min treatment, but it really lasted 60 to 65 minutes maximum. Overall, this is a resort to stay in at least once however the spa experience needs to be improved to be up to par with the resort according to our experience.

title: Excellent if you are willing to pay
review_text: I think Bvlgari Hotel Bali is more suitable for couples on honeymoon. Stayed there 2 nights for business a couple years ago. The bathroom is massive - excellent. Love the bed and all the Bvlgari amenities. Love the 6 bottles of Equil provided inside the room every single day, the dessert provided in the room as complimentary. A bit too dark during the night though - perhaps to get the romantic feeling. The terrace and private pool is awesome. Breakfast was serve in their Sangkar restaurant and it was good. Basically for the amount of money that they are charging for this villa, you would expect excellent room with excellent service and you will get it in Bvlgari. If you are staying here and wanting to travel outside the hotel, I recommended you to hire a private driver as the area is quite far away from the City area of Uluwatu. I'm sure the hotel would be able to provide drivers too but of course it would be a lot more expensive.

title: Amazing hotel and friendly staff
review_text: love almost everything of the hotel!!!. It is located 40 mins away from the airport. The villa are spacious and with massive bathroom. For sure is one of the most popular places for honeymoon, do see lots of asian couples. Since it is located a bit far from everything, so do expect to have 1-2 hour to traveling time to many tourist spots. The breakfast was ok, i was expecting a lot more than just some fruits, eggs and bread, but the bulgari brand jam was delicious!!!! The spa was fantastic, each spa room faces to the Indian ocean. But make sure you have spray with you, mosquito can be anywhere esp during rain season.

title: Almost heavenly, but not quiet
review_text: Let's get all the good stuff first. The view from our room was just sensational! We had room service for breakfast and it was excellent. Service was very good, although not as top-notch as some of the other luxury hotels in Bali. What I found to be the low point of this resort is its design. The mostly black decor does not go well with the climate in Bali and if anything, it attracts a lot of mosquitoes. The glass door going to bedroom is not the easiest to operate and the bathroom, while large, does not feel luxurious. The morning monkey sighting from our villa was a nice surprise.

title: Very good but not quite great.
review_text: We stayed at the Bulgari hotel in Sept '13. This is our fourth visit to Bali (previously stayed at Bale, Ritz Carlton, and Four Seasons) and as before thoroughly enjoyed our visit. The hotel villas are very nice and very private. They're a little dark for my taste but I think this is more of a brand-Bulgari choice rather than a fault. The TV/audio are high spec and have good channel access for the late night vegeing when back from dinner. For those who like to keep in touch with the world the wifi is excellent. The food in the Italian restaurant and the cocktails at the bar are of the highest quality but a little pricey when compared to what you can get 20-30min away in Jimbarin. The staff make the difference between most other hotels I've stayed in here in Bali with them meeting every unusual request I had. Serious thanks to them and they're a credit to the Island the hotel's management. The hotel's plus points are; 1. definitely one of the quietest hotels i've stayed at in Bali. heard zero noise, music, etc. for our neighbour villas despite being relatively close. 2. amazing views 3. very friendly staff 4. very private The negative points are; 1. mosquitoes (five or six flying around in the spa changing room) seemed more prevalent than other hotels I've stayed in. Granted this is pretty subjective. 2. food quite pricey and very little options

title: Decent Hotel; Stay elsewhere
review_text: I stayed 3 nights for a friend's wedding in Bali. Location is good; beach was closed so had to share a beach with the Marriott property. Resort itself is pretty, the villa was nice. Finishes were spectacular but a little over the top i.e.bang and olufsen TV is not necessary. A large Sony seems a lot more practical. Bathroom was nice and spacious, private dipping pool was small but functional. I feel like I paid a lot for the "bulgari" name rather than the actual hotel. A lot of it seemed a little gimmicky or obnoxious i.e. checking vehicles for bombs at the entrance gate of the hotel. One thing that was undoubtedly spectacular was their service. My butler (forgot his name) arranged everything I wanted in my suite to perfection. They also threw in a free gift everyday which was kind of nice. Two disappointing things about this hotel: Laundry service: laundry was pretty bad. Melted some of my buttons of my shirt and made a new ironing line on my trousers. Breakfast was not very good. Would not stay here again.

title: Perfectly Picturesque
review_text: The Bulgari Resort was absolutely stunning. My husband and I stayed here for the final 3 nights of our Honeymoon. We booked the cliff-side villa. It was worth it. The views were incredible and the space was completely private. We were in our own little paradise with a infinity pool right outside our door on the edge of a cliff overlooking the ocean. The resort was very tranquil and the service was excellent. The staff was extremely responsive to any request. The beach at the hotel was unfortunately closed, but we were provided free transportation to 3 nearby resort beaches and a Bulgari staff member stayed with us at our beach visits to make sure we were attended to for food and drinks. The villa itself was beautifully designed and combined traditional Balinese architecture with European elements. I must advise if staying at the resort to be sure your doors are closed and secure at all times. We must have left a door unlocked on our trip to the spa and a monkey made its way into the room and ate a platter of fruit. Luckily it had its snack and left immediately because nothing else was touched.

title: The best resort I ever stayed at !
review_text: There is nothing I can fault this resort on. I will list the things that impressed me the most: 1. All the staff were friendly and helpful. It was a good touch to be addressed personally when attending dinner and breakfast. 2. Room service was the fastest I have experienced. We requested a bucket of ice and within 5 to 10 minutes it was delivered. 3. I asked for 2 pieces of clothes to be ironed. The long pants had 2 small holes in them, before I sent them off. Room service phoned me to check if indeed they were damaged. Any other hotel would have not bothered. 4. The food and wine in the restaurant was of the highest quality. 5. The Villa was amazing Yes it's expensive but you MUST experience this resort ! The only negative was that the lift down to the beach was under maintenance.

title: Excellent Hospitality
review_text: The hospitality that was given to me and my friends were very good. They anticipated our needs, was ever ready to assist us on our sightseeing plans and arranging transport for us. A friend of ours who doesn't eat too spicy food wanted to taste a dish on a menu and the service staff and chef cooked a small bowl for my friend to try before proceeding with the ordering. They even accomodate our almost after hours ordering of room service with full menu. My friends and I felt very warm and welcome by the all the staffs gestures.

title: One of the best hotels in the world!
review_text: We travel A LOT and definitely Bvlgari Hotel is one of the best hotels in the world. I know that the fee is high but you have to understand that the quality of EVERYTHING here is the best. We came from Brazil to spend 4 days in this beautiful hotel, one week before our arrival we received a very nice email from Septi, the guest relations, explaining all the process to arrive in Bali, sending the restaurant menu among other things. She was incredible and answered all my emails very quickly arranging all we need to dive in Bali, she also offered to find anything we need during our visit (tours, restaurants, taxi, etc.) and to help us anytime. isn't it amazing? When we arrived at the airport there was a guy waiting us to make our immigration. I strongly recommend this option cause the lines are huuuuuge and it's cheap, around 30US per person, after this the transfer from the hotel (BMW X5) was waiting for us with bottles of water and delicious cookies from the hotel bakery. We arrived at the hotel and did our check in very quickly. The employees of the hotel were wonderful and they took us in a golf car to our room, inside the room they explained about the room and it's facilities and about the hotel, 24 hours room service :) I mean not only sandwiches but everything! you can have your breakfast in your room no extra charges, the room is exactly what you seed in the pics, the size is good and the amenities are perfect! they have everything, even beach bags, hats and flip flops! All the employees knew our names, they want to make it personal, they want you to feel home and they make all the necessary effort to realize all your needs. We went to the Italian restaurant all days: IL Restaurant, there is a italian called Carlos how surprised us every night with his great sense of humor and his excellent knowledge. Every day they left in our room some art craft as a gift. I have to say that we were spoiled a lot, it's great when you have so many ppl working to make your stay the most incredible as they can, you can see that they have passion about working in that hotel and this is nice to see. I strongly recommend the SPA and the yoga classes during the morning. The place looks like a temple, you will definitely love it! The only thing that I have to say that is not 100% is the location, not because of the perfect and peaceful ocean view but because it's far from most of the places you want to visit... but hey? what I am talking about? This is a hotel to forget about the world and live every single minute enjoying its facilities.

title: Its like paradise on earth
review_text: Stayed at Bvlgari hotel and resorts and was in awe upon arrival with the views and the excellent welcome team at the hotel. The service from check in till check out was really commendable. The villa that we stayed was just beyond words especially the views and the in room decoration and amenities. The view was also to die for. But one comment I have is that their bathroom is beautiful and huge but there were no doors and we were three friends sharing a room as we went a group so it was a bit inconvenient in a way. Other that that, there were lots of mosquitoes in the night so you need to bring lots of insect repellent. All in all it was a wonderful stay and would highly recommend this hotel for honeymoon couples or just to relax and get away from it all! Two thumbs up !!

title: Stunning View
review_text: The standout feature of this resort is obviously the location and view from the resort. Whilst the service staff tries their best, the overall standards are not quite perfect, though they are really minor issues such as late arrival of the buggie during checkout, we had to call the butler to re-confirm a beach trip instead of him calling back etc. The resort is getting old but well maintained. Breakfast was average. Beach remained closed but a nice trip to Finn's Beach Club was arranged by the resort. Overall a very lovely stay. But just not perfect.

title: Good but could be better!
review_text: We stayed at Bulgari during the Easter long weekend. We were glad that we used their airport transfer service as it is our first time to Bali and didn't know our way around. Bulgari staffs took care all of these for us. During our stay there, Bulgari staffs tried their best to make sure that we are happy and satisfied. One thing to note that is the buggy did take about 10 minutes to come. Maybe Bulgari should have a few more buggies to minimise the waiting time. We were running late for a few appointments because of this. I also really enjoyed the food served by both italian and Sangkar restaurants as well as in room dining. We ate at the Italian restaurant and were given complimentary breads, starter, sorbet ( to clean our palate) and chocolate truffles and turkish delight. Food was amazing and yummylicious. However, there was a monkey inside our villa on our last night in Bulgari and making such a loud noise ( right above our bed) that we couldnt sleep. We were initally told by the staffs to go back to sleep and they tried to convince us its a crab. In the end, we moved to another villla at 4.30am. Our plan to watch the sunrise and photographtaking was ditched as we were tired after this monkey drama. We stayed at villa 57, not close to the cliff at all. We had same issue that some of the reviewers had - the door is quite hard to open! We also had one of our best spa experiences here. I would conclude that my overall stay at Bulgari Bali was lovely but there is room for improvement. I will come back again providing that there is no monkey invading my villa.

title: Brilliant staff and location. Less than ordinary after that!
review_text: We loved the resort, position, position, position, HOWEVER all was not as promised. The staff were so so good, as only the Balinese can be, but surely management have to take responsibility for the many negatives that arose during our 4 night stay. I have been reading tripadvisor reviews for months about maintenance issues in the villas, we had doorknobs falling off, shower leaking, toilet constantly flowing and floorboards so badly warped that barefoot walking was a constant threat. Obviously management don't give a rats backside about guest reviews. Spa attendees are advised to arrive 40 mins early and spa menu is advertised. I arrived at 7.55 for a 8.30am appointment and waited till 8.20 for staff to arrive, no apology and during 2 visits I was not offered the menu to peruse. We were advised that late checkout was available as we had an 8.30 pm departure from the resort but all members of our group were turfed out of their rooms at 2.00 pm. We were told by many staff that occupancy was dropping considerably as our group used more than 50% of the resort, why upset paying guests for nonexistent ones??? It is only because the staff were so amazing that this review is offering a VG rating. If I return to Bali I will not be staying at Bulgari!

title: Everything you expect from a luxurious brand.
review_text: From the moment you are greeted to the moment you leave, you are in safe and caring hands. Every attention to detail is looked after and you are made to feel as special as any celebrity. The views are beautiful and the only sounds come from the ocean. The villas are very spacious in all areas, the room, the bathroom and the outdoor area. The only thing i would suggest is to include fly spray like other hotels. An insect repellant would be a nice touch too given the amount you are paying per night. Luckily we brought our own. Each room has a private pool surrounded by a lot of foliage, which could be part of the reason there are so many mosquito's. I would recommend this hotel as an experience, but there are plenty of other hotels that are just as nice for half the price. Also the buggy's that drive guests around can be quite noisy. There's not a lot to do outside of the resort, so best to make the most of your stay and enjoy the hotel and the Italian restaurant.

title: AMAZING PLACE TO VISIT
review_text: I was not fortunate enough to stay here (due to the cost) but was lucky enough to be able to visit to join friends (staying here) for lunch, the open lobby is a sight to behold you can't stop looking at the views out to the Indian Ocean with cliff top villas nestled into the hillside. Food was faultless Service was faultless and the resort is second to none, we stayed all afternoon and what a joy to be here, the view and location of Bulgari is well worth the visit. Can't wait to go back there even just to stay one night.

title: Rustic, Luxurious
review_text: We were in Bali and simply had to try out this luxury hotel for a night. We arrived at night, and we felt overwhelmed by the sheer luxury of this place. No stone was left unturned in creating a super-exclusive atmosphere. Even the security checks were longer and they even had guard dogs, which made us feel that the resort was closely guarded and thus very safe. Check-in was quick and uneventful. We absolutely loved that everything was Bulgari-branded. It was quite an experience to live within such luxury. We were brought to our villa, which was gigantic. We could not see the cliff view as it was at night but we could hear the sounds of the waves, which was very peaceful and relaxing. The villa's style is very wooden, which would be nice to those who like it but we don't really favor the rustic ambience. Floorboards creaked softly, creating an extremely rustic feel. The bed was very comfortable, and we spent most of the night at the bar having drinks and snacks, listening to the waves. In the day, we took the glass elevator down to the beach and had a few pictures, but couldn't really walk on the beach due to high tide. It's a very secluded beach and there is another beach bar on the cliff owned by Bulgari. Certainly a great place to chill and read a book if you are staying there for a longer time. Overall, a GREAT and EXTRAVAGANT hotel. 4/5 only because we don't enjoy rustic ambiences, but otherwise it would have been perfect.

title: Bulgari Bali
review_text: We just stayed for a week in the Bulgari, Bali. The service was excellent as was our accommodation. The landscaping is unique and beautiful and there is no reason to venture out in the mad house of Denpasar or Seminyak. Everything was perfect or so we thought. The only very annoying thing is that the hotel are building a chapel on the grounds next to the reception and villas. Construction commences from 9 am in the morning to 9 pm at night. The noise was relentless and can be heard throughout the grounds of the hotel. Despite requesting on several occasions for the noise to stop before 5 pm and the staff attempting to fulfil our request this didn't happen. So if you want peace and quiet and you dont want to feel like you're staying next to a construction site DONT STAY THERE FOR AT LEAST THE NEXT 6 MONTHS until works are completed and then stay in one of the best hotels in the world!

title: Incredibly beautiful!
review_text: Bulgari adds another dimension to hotel stay. The whole place is beautiful and full of character. We had a private villa which was huge and beautiful. It gave us a full sense of privacy and home feeling. The staff was very friendly and super useful. We ate at their asian restaurant all the time and had the best meals of our stay in Bali. Loved this place so much that we didnt want to leave the hotel to visit other places. Didnt regret it. Great choice for our honeymoon!

title: Overpriced
review_text: Way too much hype. For the price , might as well rent a huge villa with 4 bedrooms. Pro: 1.Amazing view of Indian Ocean 2. Friendly efficient staff 3. Good security Con: 1. The villa is too dark, seriously need to put more lights, I can't even read what's in front of me. 2. Caught so many mosquitoes, even with the 2 repellents next to the bed (safe???) 3. Can't swim in the beach. Not suitable. 4. A few lizards roaming on the ceiling, was quite worried, one might drop on my bed. 5. Have to eat in hotel. 6. Breakfast choice average.

title: Beautiful wedding reception, but disappointing service during our stay
review_text: We recently stayed 3 nights at Bvlgari. I read some reviews in trip advisor prior to the trip and was particularly concerned with the comments re mosquitoes issues. Below is a quick summary of my experience of this resort: 1. Check in -no one greeted us, we didn't get any special welcome even we arrive with the bride n groom. A staff told of husband to collect our friends passports for her as if he's a tour rep which annoyed him. She told me this is a 'tropical island' when I told her I am very allergic to mosquitoes. (I've been Bali for five times let alone other tropical islands. I know some resorts do have more mosquitoes vs others). 2. Dinner at Sakra (their casual restaurant) - restaurant was very busy and food came late for our group, they missed my side order. Quality of food was average. I don't think a indo fusion menu work well. A waiter dropped a plate and spilled everything across our table. 3. Room Deco - very clean, no issues with mosquitoes as long as we closed the door all the time. Pool we never used as its too small, monkeys run around on resort so we can't in the villa in case we get disturbed or leave food around. But very dark lighting and furnished. Furnitures Need an upgrade as they started to look worn. 4. Buggy transfer - took 20 mins to arrange a buggy to get to the restaurant on our first night. The operator made excuses as any staff made mistakes usually do. 5. Private beach - staff refused to take us down while there's a little rain, it's nice to explore on the beach, but you can't swim in it as there were lots of rocks. 6. Pool - pool attendant didn't let me take a picture until after I told him which villa we stayed. Pool was nice to swim. All of the above happened within half a day that we arrived. We discussed this with their operation manager day 2, she told us there was no excuse and very sorry for what happened. She arranged an hour massage at the spa for us which was a kind gesture. But, if we could choose, we'd very much prefer paying for the massage without having to deal with issues during a short and expensive trip. The staff was more aware of our needs after our chat with the manager, we didn't have more issues with the buggy and checkout was efficient. For the wedding, 'the water wedding' (as referred by the hotels website) by the bar area was absolutely beautiful. The ceremony was elegantly done. It's ideal for under 100 guests. We were blessed by the good weather. A firework before the reception dinner was amazing. Dinner venue was indoor which was good as rain started half way. Their band did a great job. Everyone had a great time dancing and drinking. There was also a fire show half way during dinner. The bride and groom had their own crew of photographers, which they spent the next morning taking more photos. We spent our last night dining at their Italian restaurant, food was very good and service was good. Last thing I want to add is about the location, it's very far, which makes it a very quiet and exclusive resort to stay (perfect for wedding), but it's not for people who want to eat out and explore around town unless you want to deal with the travelling.

title: Breakfast with a View!
review_text: I absolutely loved this resort. I stayed in the lowest room category which was an Ocean view pool villa which was stunninh. The dark wood tones with stunning bright blue ocean backdrop was beautiful. The resort is influenced by both Bali and Italy, with Balinese styled villas linked by meandering cobblestone footpaths reminiscent of an Italian Village. The grounds of the property are lush, colourful and simply breathtaking. The pool area is home to a number of sun lounges and cabanas to relax in, with the pool staff on-hand to provide magazines, suncream, cooling mist, aloe vera gel and of course bar service! The resort is located high on the cliff edge of Uluwatu and it has own funicular to take guests down to the Beach Club. The Beach club has sun lounges, restaurant, a bar and staff to wait on guests. The ocean is not recommended for swimming due to the rough rocks and tides. The service at the hotel was fantastic, they could not do enough for me! I enjoyed the sunset and dinner at the bar which was delicious and breakfast at Sangkar Restaurant which was a well thought out and stylish buffet. The view from my balcony table at the restaurant for breakfast was a peaceful and truly magical way to start the day.

title: Rooms a bit dated, service decent, massive resort trying to be boutiquey
review_text: Beautiful resort, but not private by any means. Tourist buses aplenty here, but because of the spread out nature of the resort, you can isolate yourself a bit more in your own room. The rooms were sadly a bit dated, as I think a lot of our floors might’ve been rotting. The bathroom was awesome and visually stunning, complete with Bulgari style designs in small accents throughout the room. We stayed in a cliffs edge view villa, and the view was magnificent. The service was a bit spotty; you can see where they try to be on top of it, but at the end of the day, there are just too many people and it doesn’t feel very personal. However, when they can, they will try to personalize for you. I was feeling under the weather and the front desk woman at the restaurant sent me some local remedies to our room just in case, and remembered to check in with me the next morning. The resort is amazing, but I think the private room pool views were even better. Also, its totally true what they say about the monkeys, holy crap they’re scary.

title: Fantastic private beach but facilities are outdated
review_text: Stayed there for 2 nights and enjoyed the views of the private beach. This secluded beach is the biggest attraction of Bulgari. The service is impeccable as described by other reviewers. However, there is a real need to refurbish the place as a lot of the facilities are wearing out. While I understand that wear and tear is part and parcel of operation, it is about time to refurbish. Food is not bad but it didn't really live up to the expectations through other reviews. A decent range of varieties but food standard could be improved. Overall it is still a great experience but minor touchups are needed for a 5 star.

title: Stunning but not perfect
review_text: Overall this place is absolutely stunning, an amazing photo everywhere you look! However, our one bedroom ocean view private villa was a little tired (in parts) and needs a bit of maintenance to bring it back to the high level it should be for $1,500 a night! The resort staff are amazing and call you by your 1st name (even when you arrive) so you are made to feel very special in every part of the resort. Il Restaurante was ok (not amazing). Went to better restaurants in Seminary (eg. Matise) We are from Melbourne Australia so we have very high standards because our Melbourne Restaurants are world class. We actually preferred the food in the less formal Indonesian Restaurant and in hind sight would have given Il Restaurante a miss. The resort guests are predominantly Asian (70%). A handful of European and we were the only Aussies (in quite a while I think). Beach club amazing, very relaxing! Lunch at the beach club restaurant was a little disappointing but the view was too die for so lower your expectations with the food and you'll enjoy it. We had lunch at the Indonesian Resuarant at Alila Resort (just down the road) which was probably the best food we had in Bali. The Resort is also breathtakingly stunning, but I would prefer to stay at the Bulgari because it's a lot more private. Overall, this is a stunning place and you will not regret coming here for a total relaxation. Sherren from Melbourne (Australia)

title: Best villa EVER!
review_text: I visited this resort back in Feb, 2015 with my boyfriend. It was a resort I have always wanted to go. Stayed for three nights and was never disappointed. They had some construction going on and I kind of heard the noise but it was not loud enough to distract me. The furnitures are a little worn out but it still is a great place! The view from the sun bed in our villa was breathtaking. Also, the service couldn't have been better. I had a stomach flu (?) while I was staying there so I could not make it to the dinner reservation. However, the butler delivered the food I was supposed to have + porridge for my stomach and I was amazed how all the food brought to me was same as what I would expect at a restaurant! Since there are hills within the resort and the butlers have to go around in carts, I did not expect the food to be delivered so nicely! I would definitely return when I am back in bali!

title: Too Dark at night
review_text: Typical Bulgari Hotel Design, empty spaces, reception area stark and uninviting, and bit too morbid for my taste. We went there for drinks and snacks which were excellent, and so was the service, but then so was the price. we had intended to go for dinner for their 7 courses pairing menu but after seeing only 4 people in the restaurant it put us off hence we went to the bar. We visited in the evening and the place is too dark, they need more lights.

title: hotel is magic but the room need to be cleaned more
review_text: We lived 3 nights at the beautiful resort, the environment of hotel and our villa is impressive. The restaurant and the bar offer good choice of food and we enjoyed our drink at the swimming pool. The only thing which is regrettable is the room clean: the first night when we arrived, we found the used staff left by other clients at our bathroom.... For a hotel at this level, it is surprising that they don't check before giving a room to client.

title: Spectacular experience at Bulgari Resort Bali
review_text: Amazing view from their cliff villa overlooking the ocean. Enjoyed our private plunge pool at the villa. 300 square meter of beauty. Open space living room facing the ocean, equipped with everything you can think of. Bathroom was huge with a bathtub and shower. Loved their electronic sensor toilet and their outdoor shower. Small little complimentary gift to guest. Super comfy bed for 2. Watching the sunset from your own villa. However,the Italian restaurant was a bit pricey. Good food and service so I can't complaint too much. Shout out to the staff for their wonderful service!! I will definitely come back again.

good hotel but location too far from town.hotel area very dark at the nite time.but every staff n worker very very friendly and very kind,excellent good staffs. rooms clean and comform.pool villa facing to the ocean.

title: Fantastic travel experience
review_text: Love the days spent in Bulgari, enjoy every day and night there. It is a good choice, if You prefer a peaceful vacation far away from crowds You enjoy the your bed in a good resort You like to talk with locals You want something luxury but still natural ...... and most of all, You lover Bulgari!

title: Nice Facility and Staff
review_text: The property is beautiful, modern and well maintained. The staff is friendly, polite and helpful. The only comments are: (a) the quality of Indonesian food served at the restaurant is poor; and (b) the hourly rate charged for hiring a hotel car and driver is way too expensive even compared to Amandari and especially given the bad traffic jam during the peak tourist season.

title: Our stay is very memorable! Service is outstanding!
review_text: The service here is outstanding you will really feel like you are taken care of and not just a room number. The location is spectacular with views of the cliff and sea. One of the things we really liked about this resort is you are treated as an individual and get very personalized service. A couple of things to be aware of you are out of nowhere from the city. A nice place for relax. All in all a lovely stay to our family.

title: Quality Everything
review_text: There aren't enough superlatives to describe the experience. Every single staff member are warm friendly genuine and endlessly helpful. Resort was as you would expect from the Bulgari brand, slick, perfectly formed and beautiful.

title: Dream stay come true!
review_text: Luxurious accommodation with a 24 hour butler service. Our butler Ada was attentive and sweet. He was very genuine and punctual. All the hotel staffs made the effort to remember little details about the guests which made us feel very at home. This property way exceeds expectations. From the reception to the buggy driver, everyone was lovely.

title: Luxury Resort
review_text: Everything is well taken care of, the view is exceptional and the service as well. The price explains its luxury so nothing unexpected. Ordered some room service food was just average. You can also notice that it's taken on quite a toll with the years of service.

title: Awesome
review_text: The stay was excellent, everyone was very helpful with anything we needed, and especially Dewa — we’re leaving Bali with a new friend. A very big thank you to him

title: Highlights of honeymoon
review_text: The breakfast at Sangkar was wonderful, and having dinner there while watching the sunset was an unforgettable experience. A special thanks to our butler Lanang, who thoughtfully reserved the perfect table for us to enjoy the sunset. The scenery was beautiful everywhere — from the main pool and pool bar where we took photos, to the stunning view from our private indoor pool. This stay was truly one of the highlights of our honeymoon, and we would love to come back again in the future. Thank you very much, Lanang, for taking such great care of us.

title: Dream week at Bvlgari Resort Dubai
review_text: A dream experience. Resort with a fantastic atmosphere, decoration, quality of mansion and villas and above all the simply spectacular service of the staff, with special emphasis on Oni and Lanang. Super professional, super available and always, always friendly at any time!!!!! Service 10 *

title: Encrible free edge pool.
review_text: Excellent adventure of 13 friends (a newly married couple)! The resort Bvlgari contemplates an urbanized area with a typical architecture carved in the landscape in sucalcos, combining local materials, with very well-treated green areas (ornamental plants of tropical origin). Silence is soothing. It has an incredibly prepared and trained reception team. Sympathy and efficiency is genuine (Ms Oni and Mr Lanang). The villas (room) are generous overlooking the Indian Ocean, breathing comfort and daily cleanliness. The pools are spacious and clear. The food at the main restaurant was varied, between Italian and Indonesian cuisine. Notable, the beach in front has strong waves, but the resort offers other leisure options. See you next time!
